scope: Port the interactive memory command and TUI memory selector into AgenC while preserving the headless slash-command fallback.

requiredBehaviors: The interactive /memory command is a local JSX command that lazy-loads the memory editor body.
The TUI command list exposes the local JSX descriptor instead of the plain dispatcher projection.
edgeCases: Headless/dispatcher callers still resolve /memory without throwing.

requiredBehaviors: The command clears and primes memory-file caches before rendering the selector.
Selecting a memory file creates the parent config directory/file when needed, opens the editor, and reports a relative display path.
The Learn more link uses the AgenC-owned documentation domain.
edgeCases: Existing files are preserved when writeFile with flag wx reports EEXIST.
EDITOR/VISUAL hints identify the active editor source.

requiredBehaviors: The selector lists existing memory files plus missing user/project memory entries.
Project memory path selection uses the MM-03 project-memory API instead of a duplicated selector-path module.
Auto-memory folders and feature-gated team memory remain folder-open options.
edgeCases: Nested imported files keep indentation and imported/dynamic descriptions.
The last selected path is reused only when it is still present.

requiredBehaviors: The update notification displays the shortest useful path relative to HOME or cwd.
The notice points users back to /memory for edits.
edgeCases: Paths outside HOME and cwd remain absolute.
